basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: Al-Raʿd — The Thunder — unfolds the cosmic signs as so many arguments for Oneness: the heavens raised without visible pillars, the sun and moon subjected, each running to an appointed term, the earth spread with its mountains and rivers, the fruits paired two by two. At the sūra's heart, the eponymous verse: “the thunder glorifies Him with His praise, and the angels, in awe, glorify Him; He sends the thunderbolts and strikes whom He wills” — Qushayrī recognizes in this, on the spiritual path, the thunderbolts that strike the aspirants through the intermittence (fatra). Then comes the great law of the soul: “God does not change the state of a people until they change what is in themselves”; then the verse of rest: “alā bi-dhikri Llāhi taṭmaʾinnu l-qulūb” — hearts find rest only in the remembrance of God — where Qushayrī distinguishes those whose quietude comes from their dhikr and those whom God Himself remembers, so that ṭumaʾnīna becomes pure favor. The sūra culminates upon the Mother of the Book (umm al-kitāb) with Him, where destinies are effaced and confirmed, and closes on the plot turned back: “to God belongs the whole plot.”

versets.ar: المر تِلْكَ آيَاتُ الْكِتَابِ وَالَّذِي أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْكَ مِنْ رَبِّكَ الْحَقُّ وَلَكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: alif lām mīm rāʾ tilka āyātu l-kitābi wa-lladhī unzila ilayka min rabbika l-ḥaqqu wa-lākinna akthara n-nāsi lā yuʾminūna
versets.trad: Alif. Lām. Mīm. Rāʾ. These are the verses of the Book. What has been sent down to you from your Lord is the Truth, but most of mankind do not believe.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads the letters as keys to the Names: alif points to Allāh, lām to al-Laṭīf (the Subtle), mīm to al-Majīd (the Glorious), rāʾ to al-Raḥīm (the Compassionate). In the name of Allāh, the Subtle, the Glorious, the Compassionate — these are the verses of the Book I have sent down upon Muḥammad. And he adds: “but most of mankind do not believe” — they are the more numerous in count, the less in rank and weight.

mots.def: keys of the divine Names per Qushayrī.

mots.racine: أ ي ي / ك ت ب
mots.sens: these are the verses of the Book

mots.racine: ن ز ل
mots.sens: what was sent down to you

mots.racine: ك ث ر / أ م ن

versets.ar: اللَّهُ الَّذِي رَفَعَ السَّمَاوَاتِ بِغَيْرِ عَمَدٍ تَرَوْنَهَا ثُمَّ اسْتَوَى عَلَى الْعَرْشِ وَسَخَّرَ الشَّمْسَ وَالْقَمَرَ كُلٌّ يَجْرِي لِأَجَلٍ مُسَمًّى يُدَبِّرُ الْأَمْرَ يُفَصِّلُ الْآيَاتِ لَعَلَّكُمْ بِلِقَاءِ رَبِّكُمْ تُوقِنُونَ
versets.tr: allāhu lladhī rafaʿa s-samāwāti bi-ghayri ʿamadin tarawnahā thumma stawā ʿalā l-ʿarshi wa-sakhkhara sh-shamsa wa-l-qamara kullun yajrī li-ajalin musamman yudabbiru l-amra yufaṣṣilu l-āyāti laʿallakum bi-liqāʾi rabbikum tūqinūna
versets.trad: God is the One who raised the heavens without pillars you can see. Then He established Himself upon the Throne. He subjected the sun and the moon, each running to an appointed term; He orders the affair; He sets forth the signs in detail — that perhaps you may be certain of the meeting with your Lord.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He attests His attributes and essence through the signs He proclaims — among them the raising of the heavens with no pillar to bear them, no stakes to hold them. His establishing upon the Throne (istawā ʿalā l-ʿarsh) means He encompasses His dominion in a grasp of power and governance. And on sun and moon: each runs in its orbit, and each particle of this attests that it is the work of a King in His dominion, without partner.

mots.racine: ر ف ع / ع م د
س و ي / ع ر ش
mots.sens: He established Himself upon the Throne
mots.def: istawā: to establish; ʿarsh: Throne, dominion.

mots.racine: س خ ر
أ ج ل / س م ي

mots.sens: you may be certain of the meeting

versets.ar: وَهُوَ الَّذِي مَدَّ الْأَرْضَ وَجَعَلَ فِيهَا رَوَاسِيَ وَأَنْهَارًا وَمِنْ كُلِّ الثَّمَرَاتِ جَعَلَ فِيهَا زَوْجَيْنِ اثْنَيْنِ يُغْشِي اللَّيْلَ النَّهَارَ إِنَّ فِي ذَلِكَ لَآيَاتٍ لِقَوْمٍ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-huwa lladhī madda l-arḍa wa-jaʿala fīhā rawāsiya wa-anhāran wa-min kulli th-thamarāti jaʿala fīhā zawjayni thnayni yughshī l-layla n-nahāra inna fī dhālika la-āyātin li-qawmin yatafakkarūna
versets.trad: It is He who spread out the earth and placed in it firm mountains and rivers, and of every kind of fruit He placed in it two pairs. He covers the day with the night. Surely in this are signs for a people who reflect.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds the work of the One who spreads: He smoothed the earth and rolled it out, planted its mountains as anchors, made its springs gush forth and rivers flow, varied its seas and the life that peoples them, made its trees grow, ordered its flowers and fruits, wrapped its night and day around it. Such is the determining of the Mighty, the All-Knowing. Each pair (zawjayn) within a fruit is a sign of a wisdom that loves to pair.

mots.racine: م د د
ر س و / ن ه ر
ز و ج
غ ش و
ف ك ر

versets.ar: وَفِي الْأَرْضِ قِطَعٌ مُتَجَاوِرَاتٌ وَجَنَّاتٌ مِنْ أَعْنَابٍ وَزَرْعٌ وَنَخِيلٌ صِنْوَانٌ وَغَيْرُ صِنْوَانٍ يُسْقَى بِمَاءٍ وَاحِدٍ وَنُفَضِّلُ بَعْضَهَا عَلَى بَعْضٍ فِي الْأُكُلِ إِنَّ فِي ذَلِكَ لَآيَاتٍ لِقَوْمٍ يَعْقِلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-fī l-arḍi qiṭaʿun mutajāwirātun wa-jannātun min aʿnābin wa-zarʿun wa-nakhīlun ṣinwānun wa-ghayru ṣinwānin yusqā bi-māʾin wāḥidin wa-nufaḍḍilu baʿḍahā ʿalā baʿḍin fī l-ukuli inna fī dhālika la-āyātin li-qawmin yaʿqilūna
versets.trad: On the earth are neighboring plots, gardens of vines, crops, and palm trees, clustered or single, all watered by one same water; yet some We make more excellent than others in fruit. Surely in this are signs for a people who reason.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī meditates unity in diversity: plots salty, stony, sandy — diverse kinds, matched pairs. Crops, plants, varied trees, whose origin is one; their parts resemble one another, their portions correspond — one is sap, another bark, one branch, another trunk, one flower, another leaf. All is one, yet each receives its own nature, form, color and bark, though watered by a single water that reaches each part according to its need. Mystery of “We favor some over others.”

mots.racine: ق ط ع / ج و ر
ن خ ل / ص ن و
mots.def: ṣinwān: many trunks on one root.

mots.racine: س ق ي / و ح د

mots.ar: نُفَضِّلُ بَعْضَهَا عَلَى بَعْضٍ فِي الْأُكُلِ
mots.tr: nufaḍḍilu baʿḍahā ʿalā baʿḍin fī l-ukul
mots.racine: ف ض ل / أ ك ل
mots.sens: We favor some over others in fruit

mots.racine: ع ق ل

versets.ar: وَإِنْ تَعْجَبْ فَعَجَبٌ قَوْلُهُمْ أَإِذَا كُنَّا تُرَابًا أَإِنَّا لَفِي خَلْقٍ جَدِيدٍ أُولَئِكَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بِرَبِّهِمْ وَأُولَئِكَ الْأَغْلَالُ فِي أَعْنَاقِهِمْ وَأُولَئِكَ أَصْحَابُ النَّارِ هُمْ فِيهَا خَالِدُونَ
versets.tr: wa-in taʿjab fa-ʿajabun qawluhum a-idhā kunnā turāban a-innā la-fī khalqin jadīdin ulāʾika lladhīna kafarū bi-rabbihim wa-ulāʾika l-aghlālu fī aʿnāqihim wa-ulāʾika aṣḥābu n-nāri hum fīhā khālidūna
versets.trad: If you marvel, then marvel indeed at their words: “When we are dust, shall we then be in a new creation?” Those are the ones who disbelieved in their Lord; on their necks shall be the yokes; they are the inmates of the Fire, abiding in it forever.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī pauses on the very nature of marveling: marveling cannot be said of the Real's attribute, for to marvel is to deem remote — and He deems nothing remote. But here marveling is set for the creature; and it has been well said: “one marvels only at what veils” — for whoever has been given the eyes of inner sight no longer marvels at anything. The denial of the second beginning contradicts the admission of the first: had not God cast a veil upon them, the matter could not have stayed hidden from them given its evidence.

mots.racine: ع ج ب / ق و ل
mots.sens: if you marvel, their word is marvel

mots.racine: ت ر ب
غ ل ل / ع ن ق
ص ح ب / ن و ر
mots.def: aṣḥāb al-nār: companions of the Fire.

mots.racine: خ ل د

versets.ar: وَيَسْتَعْجِلُونَكَ بِالسَّيِّئَةِ قَبْلَ الْحَسَنَةِ وَقَدْ خَلَتْ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمُ الْمَثُلَاتُ وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَذُو مَغْفِرَةٍ لِلنَّاسِ عَلَى ظُلْمِهِمْ وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَشَدِيدُ الْعِقَابِ
versets.tr: wa-yastaʿjilūnaka bi-s-sayyiʾati qabla l-ḥasanati wa-qad khalat min qablihimu l-mathulātu wa-inna rabbaka la-dhū maghfiratin li-n-nāsi ʿalā ẓulmihim wa-inna rabbaka la-shadīdu l-ʿiqābi
versets.trad: They press you to hasten the evil before the good, though exemplary punishments have come to pass before them. Truly your Lord is full of forgiveness for mankind despite their wrongdoing, yet your Lord is severe in retribution.
versets.commentaire: Folly of those who call down chastisement before grace: they hasten toward what will undo them. Yet the balance holds: He is endowed with forgiveness (maghfira) for mankind despite their wrongdoing — and He is severe in retribution (shadīd al-ʿiqāb). Qushayrī sees in this double face the divine secret: mercy precedes, but justice is not in vain. The examples of past peoples (al-mathulāt) stand there for whoever would learn.

mots.racine: ع ج ل / س و أ
mots.sens: they press you for the evil

versets.ar: وَيَقُولُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لَوْلَا أُنْزِلَ عَلَيْهِ آيَةٌ مِنْ رَبِّهِ إِنَّمَا أَنْتَ مُنْذِرٌ وَلِكُلِّ قَوْمٍ هَادٍ
versets.tr: wa-yaqūlu lladhīna kafarū lawlā unzila ʿalayhi āyatun min rabbihi innamā anta mundhirun wa-li-kulli qawmin hādin
versets.trad: The disbelievers say: “If only a sign had been sent down upon him from his Lord!” You are only a warner, and for every people there is a guide.
versets.commentaire: The disbelievers demand a spectacular sign; God recalls that the Prophet's mission is warning (indhār), not miracle on demand. “For every people a guide”: Qushayrī hears here the divine solicitude that has left no community without the lamp that leads to Him. The Prophet is warner, but through him the chain of guides extends to every generation.

mots.racine: ن ز ل / أ ي ي
ن ذ ر
ه د ي

versets.ar: اللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ مَا تَحْمِلُ كُلُّ أُنْثَى وَمَا تَغِيضُ الْأَرْحَامُ وَمَا تَزْدَادُ وَكُلُّ شَيْءٍ عِنْدَهُ بِمِقْدَارٍ
versets.tr: allāhu yaʿlamu mā taḥmilu kullu unthā wa-mā taghīḍu l-arḥāmu wa-mā tazdādu wa-kullu shayʾin ʿindahu bi-miqdārin
versets.trad: God knows what every female bears, what the wombs lessen and what they increase. Everything with Him is by measure.
versets.commentaire: Total knowledge of the Unseen: God knows what wombs bear, what diminishes in them, what increases. Everything is “by measure” (bi-miqdār): nothing escapes His weighing. Qushayrī recognizes here the trace of the Decree (qadar): what appears as chance or defect in the world of wombs is in truth adjusted to the eternal knowledge; the woman's womb is already a page of the Book.

mots.racine: ح م ل / أ ن ث

versets.ar: عَالِمُ الْغَيْبِ وَالشَّهَادَةِ الْكَبِيرُ الْمُتَعَالِ
versets.tr: ʿālimu l-ghaybi wa-sh-shahādati l-kabīru l-mutaʿāli
versets.trad: Knower of the unseen and the witnessed, the Most Great, the Most High.
versets.commentaire: Three Names meet: ʿĀlim al-ghayb wa-l-shahāda (Knower of the Hidden and the Manifest), al-Kabīr (the Infinitely Great), al-Mutaʿāl (the Most High). The divine knowledge has no outside: what eludes human gaze — the ghayb — is for Him presence. And His greatness transcends every measure: al-Mutaʿāl bespeaks that elevation which makes all comparison vain.

mots.racine: ع ل م / غ ي ب / ش ه د
mots.sens: Knower of the Hidden and Manifest

mots.racine: ك ب ر / ع ل و

versets.ar: سَوَاءٌ مِنْكُمْ مَنْ أَسَرَّ الْقَوْلَ وَمَنْ جَهَرَ بِهِ وَمَنْ هُوَ مُسْتَخْفٍ بِاللَّيْلِ وَسَارِبٌ بِالنَّهَارِ
versets.tr: sawāʾun minkum man asarra l-qawla wa-man jahara bihi wa-man huwa mustakhfin bi-l-layli wa-sāribun bi-n-nahāri
versets.trad: It is alike [to Him] among you whoever keeps his speech secret and whoever proclaims it, whoever hides by night and whoever moves openly by day.
versets.commentaire: For Him, whisper and shout are equal (sawāʾ); the hidden of the night and the openness of the day, identical. Qushayrī hears here a school of inner modesty: whoever knows that his secret word reaches God as much as his public one has no more backstage. For the truthful servant there is no scene where one stands outside the divine gaze — all of life becomes an illumined brow.

mots.racine: س و ي
س ر ر / ق و ل
ج ه ر
خ ف ي / ل ي ل
س ر ب / ن ه ر

versets.ar: لَهُ مُعَقِّبَاتٌ مِنْ بَيْنِ يَدَيْهِ وَمِنْ خَلْفِهِ يَحْفَظُونَهُ مِنْ أَمْرِ اللَّهِ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يُغَيِّرُ مَا بِقَوْمٍ حَتَّى يُغَيِّرُوا مَا بِأَنْفُسِهِمْ وَإِذَا أَرَادَ اللَّهُ بِقَوْمٍ سُوءًا فَلَا مَرَدَّ لَهُ وَمَا لَهُمْ مِنْ دُونِهِ مِنْ وَالٍ
versets.tr: lahu muʿaqqibātun min bayni yadayhi wa-min khalfihi yaḥfaẓūnahu min amri llāhi inna llāha lā yughayyiru mā bi-qawmin ḥattā yughayyirū mā bi-anfusihim wa-idhā arāda llāhu bi-qawmin sūʾan fa-lā maradda lahu wa-mā lahum min dūnihi min wālin
versets.trad: Every person has angels in succession before him and behind him, guarding him by God's command. God does not change the state of a people until they change what is in themselves. When God wills evil for a people, there is no repelling it, and they have no protector apart from Him.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī magnificently unfolds the law of change: if they change what is in themselves toward obedience, God changes what is with them into favor and grace; if they were in grace and change their gratitude, He withdraws what He had given. And it is said: if they change what is on their tongues — the dhikr — God changes what is in their hearts, substituting forgetfulness and inadvertence for remembrance. And he adds: when the servant is in expansion and nearness, in the heart's unveiling and watchfulness — God changes what is in him only through a lapse of courtesy, a breach of a right, or the brush of a sin.

mots.racine: ع ق ب
ح ف ظ / أ م ر
mots.sens: they guard him by God's command

mots.sens: until they change what is in themselves

versets.ar: هُوَ الَّذِي يُرِيكُمُ الْبَرْقَ خَوْفًا وَطَمَعًا وَيُنْشِئُ السَّحَابَ الثِّقَالَ
versets.tr: huwa lladhī yurīkumu l-barqa khawfan wa-ṭamaʿan wa-yunshiʾu s-saḥāba th-thiqāla
versets.trad: It is He who shows you the lightning, inspiring fear and hope, and who brings forth the heavy clouds.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī moves from outer lightning to the lightning of the secrets: just as He shows them the lightning in the visible — and they stand between fear (of withheld rain, or of rain harming the traveler) and hope (of rain that profits the dweller) — so He shows them the lightning in their secrets, through what rises there as glimmers (lawāʾiḥ), then flashes (lawāmiʿ), then like the pure lightning: these are the lights of presence (muḥāḍara), then the lights of unveiling (mukāshafa). And he adds: “fear” lest it be cut short; “hope” that it endure and carry from presence to unveiling, then from unveiling to contemplation (mushāhada), then to existence, to the perdurance of existence, and finally to the perfection of extinction.

mots.racine: ر أ ي / ب ر ق
mots.def: arā: to make see; barq: lightning.

mots.racine: خ و ف / ط م ع
ن ش أ / س ح ب / ث ق ل

versets.ar: وَيُسَبِّحُ الرَّعْدُ بِحَمْدِهِ وَالْمَلَائِكَةُ مِنْ خِيفَتِهِ وَيُرْسِلُ الصَّوَاعِقَ فَيُصِيبُ بِهَا مَنْ يَشَاءُ وَهُمْ يُجَادِلُونَ فِي اللَّهِ وَهُوَ شَدِيدُ الْمِحَالِ
versets.tr: wa-yusabbiḥu r-raʿdu bi-ḥamdihi wa-l-malāʾikatu min khīfatihi wa-yursilu ṣ-ṣawāʿiqa fa-yuṣību bihā man yashāʾu wa-hum yujādilūna fī llāhi wa-huwa shadīdu l-miḥāli
versets.trad: The thunder glorifies Him with His praise, and the angels, in awe of Him; He sends the thunderbolts and strikes with them whom He wills, while they dispute about God; and He is mighty in stratagem.
versets.commentaire: The sūra's eponymous verse: thunder is cosmic tasbīḥ, and the angels glorify in awe. Qushayrī enters the heart of the murīd: there are sometimes in the heart a moaning and a groan, a sigh and a sob. And when the angels glimpse the hearts of the aspirants — especially — they weep blood for them, above all when there falls upon one of them an intermittence (fatra): and the intermittence, on this Path, is the thunderbolts with which He strikes whom He wills. And he cites: “What You bestowed upon us of Your union was but a lamp that flashed, then went out.”

mots.racine: س ب ح / ر ع د / ح م د
mots.sens: the thunder glorifies with His praise

mots.racine: م ل ك / خ و ف
mots.sens: and the angels in awe of Him

versets.ar: لَهُ دَعْوَةُ الْحَقِّ وَالَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ مِنْ دُونِهِ لَا يَسْتَجِيبُونَ لَهُمْ بِشَيْءٍ إِلَّا كَبَاسِطِ كَفَّيْهِ إِلَى الْمَاءِ لِيَبْلُغَ فَاهُ وَمَا هُوَ بِبَالِغِهِ وَمَا دُعَاءُ الْكَافِرِينَ إِلَّا فِي ضَلَالٍ
versets.tr: lahu daʿwatu l-ḥaqqi wa-lladhīna yadʿūna min dūnihi lā yastajībūna lahum bi-shayʾin illā ka-bāsiṭi kaffayhi ilā l-māʾi li-yablugha fāhu wa-mā huwa bi-bālighihi wa-mā duʿāʾu l-kāfirīna illā fī ḍalālin
versets.trad: To Him belongs the true call. Those they call upon besides Him answer them with nothing — as one who stretches his palms toward water that it may reach his mouth, but it never reaches it. The disbelievers' invocation is only in error.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes three callings: the callings of the Real appear in hearts by way of proof; whoever listens with the hearing of understanding answers with the clarity of knowledge. Opposite arise the callings of the Devil, which summon to adorn transgressions; whoever listens with the hearing of inadvertence answers the voice of error. And with them, the callings of the soul (nafs), which lead the servant by the reins of personal shares; whoever leans toward them falls into the humiliation of the veil. But: the callings of the Real are without angelic intermediary, without guide of intellect, without sign of knowledge; whoever the Real makes hear them, that one inevitably answers — to God by God.

ب س ط / ك ف ف
mots.sens: like one stretching his palms to water

versets.ar: وَلِلَّهِ يَسْجُدُ مَنْ فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ طَوْعًا وَكَرْهًا وَظِلَالُهُمْ بِالْغُدُوِّ وَالْآصَالِ
versets.tr: wa-li-llāhi yasjudu man fī s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi ṭawʿan wa-karhan wa-ẓilāluhum bi-l-ghuduwwi wa-l-āṣāli
versets.trad: To God prostrates whoever is in the heavens and the earth, willingly or unwillingly, and their shadows too, mornings and evenings.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes two prostrations: the soul's prostration is conventional, while the heart's prostration is of the order of finding (wujūd) — and there is a difference between one who is with his soul and one who finds with his heart. And he adds: all prostrate to God, either by acts through choice, or by states under the seal of indigence and good tidings: prostration by way of indication of Oneness — every particle of being or trace testifies to Oneness, and in this sense all prostrate to God; prostration by way of witness to the Maker's power and His dignity in bearing the attributes of Majesty.

mots.racine: س ج د
mots.sens: prostrates whoever is in heavens and earth

mots.racine: ط و ع / ك ر ه
ظ ل ل / غ د و / أ ص ل

versets.ar: قُلْ مَنْ رَبُّ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ قُلِ اللَّهُ قُلْ أَفَاتَّخَذْتُمْ مِنْ دُونِهِ أَوْلِيَاءَ لَا يَمْلِكُونَ لِأَنْفُسِهِمْ نَفْعًا وَلَا ضَرًّا قُلْ هَلْ يَسْتَوِي الْأَعْمَى وَالْبَصِيرُ أَمْ هَلْ تَسْتَوِي الظُّلُمَاتُ وَالنُّورُ أَمْ جَعَلُوا لِلَّهِ شُرَكَاءَ خَلَقُوا كَخَلْقِهِ فَتَشَابَهَ الْخَلْقُ عَلَيْهِمْ قُلِ اللَّهُ خَالِقُ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ وَهُوَ الْوَاحِدُ الْقَهَّارُ
versets.tr: qul man rabbu s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi quli llāhu qul a-fa-ttakhadhtum min dūnihi awliyāʾa lā yamlikūna li-anfusihim nafʿan wa-lā ḍarran qul hal yastawī l-aʿmā wa-l-baṣīru am hal tastawī ẓ-ẓulumātu wa-n-nūru am jaʿalū li-llāhi shurakāʾa khalaqū ka-khalqihi fa-tashābaha l-khalqu ʿalayhim quli llāhu khāliqu kulli shayʾin wa-huwa l-wāḥidu l-qahhāru
versets.trad: Say: “Who is the Lord of the heavens and the earth?” Say: “God.” Say: “Have you then taken besides Him protectors who possess for themselves no benefit and no harm?” Say: “Are the blind and the seeing alike? Or are darkness and light alike?” Or have they set up partners for God who have created as He has created, so the creations resembled each other to them? Say: “God is the Creator of all things, and He is the One, the Overpowering.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the blind is one upon whose inner sight there lies a film and a veil; the seeing is one whose secret has been anointed by the Real with the kohl of Oneness — they are not alike. And do the darknesses of association equal the lights of Oneness? And among the lights is the going out toward the brightness of contemplating the Decree. On al-Wāḥid al-Qahhār: the One without substitute or replacement, whose grace is exempt from any other grace; He suffices each, and each finds strength in Him. The Overpowering (al-Qahhār): the One against whose decision in His dominion no soul slips by.

mots.racine: ر ب ب
mots.sens: who is Lord of heavens and earth

mots.racine: و ل ي
س و ي / ع م ي / ب ص ر
mots.sens: is the blind equal to the seeing?

mots.racine: ظ ل م / ن و ر
و ح د / ق ه ر

versets.ar: أَنْزَلَ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ مَاءً فَسَالَتْ أَوْدِيَةٌ بِقَدَرِهَا فَاحْتَمَلَ السَّيْلُ زَبَدًا رَابِيًا وَمِمَّا يُوقِدُونَ عَلَيْهِ فِي النَّارِ ابْتِغَاءَ حِلْيَةٍ أَوْ مَتَاعٍ زَبَدٌ مِثْلُهُ كَذَلِكَ يَضْرِبُ اللَّهُ الْحَقَّ وَالْبَاطِلَ فَأَمَّا الزَّبَدُ فَيَذْهَبُ جُفَاءً وَأَمَّا مَا يَنْفَعُ النَّاسَ فَيَمْكُثُ فِي الْأَرْضِ كَذَلِكَ يَضْرِبُ اللَّهُ الْأَمْثَالَ
versets.tr: anzala mina s-samāʾi māʾan fa-sālat awdiyatun bi-qadarihā fa-ḥtamala s-saylu zabadan rābiyan wa-mimmā yūqidūna ʿalayhi fī n-nāri btighāʾa ḥilyatin aw matāʿin zabadun mithluhu kadhālika yaḍribu llāhu l-ḥaqqa wa-l-bāṭila fa-ammā z-zabadu fa-yadhhabu jufāʾan wa-ammā mā yanfaʿu n-nāsa fa-yamkuthu fī l-arḍi kadhālika yaḍribu llāhu l-amthāla
versets.trad: He sends down water from the sky and valleys flow according to their measure. The torrent carries a swelling froth. From what they melt in the fire, seeking ornament or utensil, a like froth arises. Thus does God strike likenesses of truth and falsehood. As for the froth, it goes off as scum; what benefits people remains on the earth. Thus does God set forth parables.
themes: the sky's water and the froth
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī decodes the great parable: God likens the Qurʾān sent down to water sent down from the sky; hearts to valleys; the devil's whisperings and the soul's promptings to the froth that floats; the True to pure substances (gold, silver, copper); the false to the dross of these substances. Just as valleys differ in smallness and greatness and carry water by their capacity — so hearts differ in receptivity by weakness or strength. And just as the torrent purifies the valley, so the Qurʾān, retained in hearts, drives the whisperings away from them. And he adds: when the lights sparkle in hearts, they repel the traces of toil; the light of certainty (yaqīn) drives off the darkness of doubt; knowledge drives off the imputation of ignorance; the light of gnosis drives off the trace of unknowing; the light of contemplation drives off the traces of the human.

mots.racine: ن ز ل / م و ه
mots.sens: He sent down water from the sky

mots.racine: و د ي / ق د ر
ز ب د / ر ب و
ض ر ب / ح ق ق / ب ط ل
mots.def: ḍaraba mathalan: to set forth a parable.

mots.racine: ذ ه ب / ج ف أ

versets.ar: لِلَّذِينَ اسْتَجَابُوا لِرَبِّهِمُ الْحُسْنَى وَالَّذِينَ لَمْ يَسْتَجِيبُوا لَهُ لَوْ أَنَّ لَهُمْ مَا فِي الْأَرْضِ جَمِيعًا وَمِثْلَهُ مَعَهُ لَافْتَدَوْا بِهِ أُولَئِكَ لَهُمْ سُوءُ الْحِسَابِ وَمَأْوَاهُمْ جَهَنَّمُ وَبِئْسَ الْمِهَادُ
versets.tr: li-lladhīna stajābū li-rabbihimu l-ḥusnā wa-lladhīna lam yastajībū lahu law anna lahum mā fī l-arḍi jamīʿan wa-mithlahu maʿahu la-ftadaw bihi ulāʾika lahum sūʾu l-ḥisābi wa-maʾwāhum jahannamu wa-biʾsa l-mihādu
versets.trad: For those who responded to their Lord is the most beautiful. As for those who did not respond — even if they had all that is on the earth and the like with it, they would offer it as ransom. Theirs is an evil reckoning, and their refuge is Hell — what a wretched bed!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “the most beautiful” (al-ḥusnā) is the promise of the acceptance of their response — and that is, for lovers, the most precious of things, for nothing is dearer to the lover than the beloved's acceptance of something from him. As for those who do not respond, were they knowingly to spend the whole earth, it would not be accepted from them: theirs is the evil reckoning (sūʾ al-ḥisāb), the minute interrogation, and after that Hell and the abiding torment.

mots.racine: ج و ب
ح س ن
ف د ي
mots.sens: they would offer it as ransom

mots.racine: س و أ / ح س ب
ب أ س / م ه د

versets.ar: أَفَمَنْ يَعْلَمُ أَنَّمَا أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْكَ مِنْ رَبِّكَ الْحَقُّ كَمَنْ هُوَ أَعْمَى إِنَّمَا يَتَذَكَّرُ أُولُو الْأَلْبَابِ
versets.tr: a-fa-man yaʿlamu annamā unzila ilayka min rabbika l-ḥaqqu ka-man huwa aʿmā innamā yatadhakkaru ulū l-albābi
versets.trad: Is he who knows that what has been sent down to you from your Lord is the Truth like one who is blind? Only those of inner understanding take heed,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads the interrogation as negation: the seeing is not equal to the blind; the accepted is not equal to the one driven back by the veil; the one hoped for through nearness is not equal to the one exposed to chastisement; the one We have set far from Our witnesses is not equal to the one We have guided by Our existence. Only one whose intellect is honor takes heed, not one whose intellect is a cause of estrangement and rebuke.

mots.racine: ع ل م
ع م ي
ذ ك ر / ل ب ب
mots.sens: only those of understanding take heed

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ يُوفُونَ بِعَهْدِ اللَّهِ وَلَا يَنْقُضُونَ الْمِيثَاقَ
versets.tr: alladhīna yūfūna bi-ʿahdi llāhi wa-lā yanquḍūna l-mīthāqa
versets.trad: those who fulfill God's covenant and do not break the pact,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: fidelity to the covenant is by the perdurance of gnosis (ʿirfān), by the respect of the condition of excellence (iḥsān), and by guarding against falling into transgression — by this was the contract sealed on the Day of the Pact (mīthāq) and the pledge. And he adds: some have taken as covenant to worship nothing but Him; others, to ask nothing of any but Him.

mots.racine: و ف ي / ع ه د
ن ق ض / و ث ق

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ يَصِلُونَ مَا أَمَرَ اللَّهُ بِهِ أَنْ يُوصَلَ وَيَخْشَوْنَ رَبَّهُمْ وَيَخَافُونَ سُوءَ الْحِسَابِ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna yaṣilūna mā amara llāhu bihi an yūṣala wa-yakhshawna rabbahum wa-yakhāfūna sūʾa l-ḥisābi
versets.trad: those who join what God has commanded to be joined, who are in awe of their Lord and fear the evil reckoning,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī beautifully unfolds the verse in three readings: “those who join”: faith in Him to faith in the prophets and messengers. And it is said: those who join their breaths one to another, so that no breath comes between for other than God, or by other than God, or in the contemplation of other than God. And it is said: they join their day's march to their night's journey in the practice of servanthood and the disowning of all power and might in themselves. And on awe (khashya): it is the bridle that stops the believer from galloping in the fields of passion, and the rein that draws him to the abiding rule of piety (taqwā).

mots.ar: يَصِلُونَ مَا أَمَرَ اللَّهُ بِهِ أَنْ يُوصَلَ
mots.tr: yaṣilūna mā amara llāhu bihi an yūṣal
mots.racine: و ص ل / أ م ر
mots.sens: they join what He commanded to be joined

mots.racine: خ ش ي
mots.sens: they are in awe of their Lord

mots.racine: س و أ / ح س ب

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ صَبَرُوا ابْتِغَاءَ وَجْهِ رَبِّهِمْ وَأَقَامُوا الصَّلَاةَ وَأَنْفَقُوا مِمَّا رَزَقْنَاهُمْ سِرًّا وَعَلَانِيَةً وَيَدْرَءُونَ بِالْحَسَنَةِ السَّيِّئَةَ أُولَئِكَ لَهُمْ عُقْبَى الدَّارِ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna ṣabarū btighāʾa wajhi rabbihim wa-aqāmū ṣ-ṣalāta wa-anfaqū mimmā razaqnāhum sirran wa-ʿalāniyatan wa-yadraʾūna bi-l-ḥasanati s-sayyiʾata ulāʾika lahum ʿuqbā d-dāri
versets.trad: those who endure with patience seeking the Face of their Lord, who establish prayer, who spend from what We have provided them, secretly and openly, and who repel evil with good — theirs is the final abode,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī grades the patiences: patience varies according to the aims for which one is patient: the devotees (ʿubbād) are patient out of fear of punishment; the renouncers (zuhhād), out of hope for reward; the people of desire (ahl al-irāda) are those who are patient seeking the Face of their Lord — and the condition of this kind of patience is to reject all that prevents arrival, and to guard against it continually. On spending: the rich spend their wealth; the devotees, their souls; the aspirants, their hearts; and the lovers — they — spend their spirits.

mots.racine: ص ب ر / ب غ ي / و ج ه
mots.sens: they endured seeking their Lord's Face

mots.racine: ن ف ق / ر ز ق
mots.sens: they spent of what We provided

د ر أ / ح س ن / س و أ
ع ق ب / د و ر

versets.ar: جَنَّاتُ عَدْنٍ يَدْخُلُونَهَا وَمَنْ صَلَحَ مِنْ آبَائِهِمْ وَأَزْوَاجِهِمْ وَذُرِّيَّاتِهِمْ وَالْمَلَائِكَةُ يَدْخُلُونَ عَلَيْهِمْ مِنْ كُلِّ بَابٍ
versets.tr: jannātu ʿadnin yadkhulūnahā wa-man ṣalaḥa min ābāʾihim wa-azwājihim wa-dhurriyyātihim wa-l-malāʾikatu yadkhulūna ʿalayhim min kulli bābin
versets.trad: the Gardens of Eden, which they enter along with the righteous among their fathers, their spouses, and their offspring; and the angels come in to them through every gate.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He perfects the grace upon them by reuniting them with those whose company they love among their kin and spouses. It has come in the tradition: “a person is with the one he loves.” He whose beloved is among his peers and kin is gathered with them; he who is, today, with his heart with God, will be tomorrow with God. And in the tradition: “I am the companion of whoever remembers Me” — that for the immediate; and for the deferred: “the patient poor will be the companions of God on the Day of Resurrection.”

mots.racine: ج ن ن / ع د ن
ص ل ح / أ ب و
د خ ل / ب و ب

versets.ar: سَلَامٌ عَلَيْكُمْ بِمَا صَبَرْتُمْ فَنِعْمَ عُقْبَى الدَّارِ
versets.tr: salāmun ʿalaykum bimā ṣabartum fa-niʿma ʿuqbā d-dāri
versets.trad: “Peace be upon you for what you endured!” — how excellent is the final abode!
versets.commentaire: The angels greet the patient with salām: the patience of this world is overturned into eternal peace. Qushayrī lets the simple beauty of the verse resonate: how excellent the issue of the abode! What was endurance in the night of trial becomes a luminous greeting at the threshold of the Garden.

mots.racine: س ل م
ص ب ر
ن ع م / ع ق ب

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ يَنْقُضُونَ عَهْدَ اللَّهِ مِنْ بَعْدِ مِيثَاقِهِ وَيَقْطَعُونَ مَا أَمَرَ اللَّهُ بِهِ أَنْ يُوصَلَ وَيُفْسِدُونَ فِي الْأَرْضِ أُولَئِكَ لَهُمُ اللَّعْنَةُ وَلَهُمْ سُوءُ الدَّارِ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna yanquḍūna ʿahda llāhi min baʿdi mīthāqihi wa-yaqṭaʿūna mā amara llāhu bihi an yūṣala wa-yufsidūna fī l-arḍi ulāʾika lahumu l-laʿnatu wa-lahum sūʾu d-dāri
versets.trad: Those who break God's covenant after their pledge, who cut what God has commanded to be joined, and who spread corruption on the earth — upon them is the curse, and theirs is the evil abode.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes two apostasies: whoever disbelieves after having believed breaks the covenant of Islam outwardly; whoever returns to the rulings of habit after having walked the path of spiritual desire breaks his covenant inwardly. The former is openly an apostate, the latter secretly so — the punishment of the first is decapitation, of the second the severing of the secret. And he adds: breaking the covenant is to seek help in others-than-Him and to abandon being content with the Almighty; it is to return to experimentation and calculation after the contemplation of the decrees.

mots.racine: ن ق ض / ع ه د

versets.ar: اللَّهُ يَبْسُطُ الرِّزْقَ لِمَنْ يَشَاءُ وَيَقْدِرُ وَفَرِحُوا بِالْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا وَمَا الْحَيَاةُ الدُّنْيَا فِي الْآخِرَةِ إِلَّا مَتَاعٌ
versets.tr: allāhu yabsuṭu r-rizqa li-man yashāʾu wa-yaqdiru wa-fariḥū bi-l-ḥayāti d-dunyā wa-mā l-ḥayātu d-dunyā fī l-ākhirati illā matāʿun
versets.trad: God extends provision to whom He wills, or measures it out. They rejoice in the life of this world — yet what is the life of this world compared to the Hereafter but a passing enjoyment?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī opens the two sides: He extends subsistence to the rich and asks of them gratitude; He narrows it on the poor and asks of them patience. To the grateful He promises increase; to the patient He promises companionship. To the rich, wealth and its growth; to the poor, stripping bare, in both abodes, of the path of their lineage and inheritance. And he continues, exquisite: the riches of the rich, however many, are little compared to what God has promised them of the existence of His favors; the states of the poor, however pure, are little compared to what He has promised them of contemplating His beauty and His majesty.

mots.racine: ب س ط / ر ز ق
ق د ر
ف ر ح / ح ي ي

versets.ar: وَيَقُولُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لَوْلَا أُنْزِلَ عَلَيْهِ آيَةٌ مِنْ رَبِّهِ قُلْ إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُضِلُّ مَنْ يَشَاءُ وَيَهْدِي إِلَيْهِ مَنْ أَنَابَ
versets.tr: wa-yaqūlu lladhīna kafarū lawlā unzila ʿalayhi āyatun min rabbihi qul inna llāha yuḍillu man yashāʾu wa-yahdī ilayhi man anāba
versets.trad: The disbelievers say: “If only a sign had been sent down upon him from his Lord!” Say: “God leads astray whom He wills and guides to Himself whoever turns back to Him,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “He leads astray whom He wills” — those who have not contemplated what was given to our Prophet of witnesses and proofs. “And He guides whom He wills” — those who saw, with the eyes of their secrets, what was singularly granted him of lights; they dwelt in the light of their inner sight. Guidance goes to the munīb, the one who turns back — not by acquired merit, but by that inclination of the heart that answers the pull from Above.

mots.racine: ن ز ل / أ ي ي
ض ل ل / ش ي أ
mots.sens: He leads astray whom He wills

mots.racine: ه د ي / ن و ب
mots.sens: He guides to Himself whoever returns

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَتَطْمَئِنُّ قُلُوبُهُمْ بِذِكْرِ اللَّهِ أَلَا بِذِكْرِ اللَّهِ تَطْمَئِنُّ الْقُلُوبُ
versets.tr: alladhīna āmanū wa-taṭmaʾinnu qulūbuhum bi-dhikri llāhi alā bi-dhikri llāhi taṭmaʾinnu l-qulūbu
versets.trad: those who believe and whose hearts find rest in the remembrance of God — surely, by the remembrance of God hearts find rest.”
versets.commentaire: The sūra's heart-verse. Qushayrī unfolds two levels: there is a people whose hearts find quietude through their remembrance of God — in remembrance they have found their solace, and through remembrance they have reached their election. And there is a people whose hearts find quietude through God's remembrance of them — meaning that God Himself remembers them in His subtlety, and establishes the quietude (ṭumaʾnīna) in their hearts as a particular election. And he adds, magnificent: if the servant does not find his heart's quietude in the remembrance of God, that is a defect in his heart — his heart is no longer among the sound hearts.

mots.racine: ط م ن / ق ل ب / ذ ك ر
mots.sens: their hearts find rest in God's remembrance
mots.def: iṭmaʾanna: to find quiet; dhikr: remembrance.

mots.racine: ذ ك ر / ق ل ب
mots.sens: is it not in God's remembrance hearts find rest?

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ طُوبَى لَهُمْ وَحُسْنُ مَآبٍ
versets.tr: alladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāti ṭūbā lahum wa-ḥusnu maʾābin
versets.trad: Those who believe and do righteous deeds — blessedness is theirs, and a beautiful place of return.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī condenses in a few words: their moments become delicious, their souls become delicious. And it is said: blessed is the one to whom the Real says “blessed.” To them blessedness in the state, and the beautiful return at the end. The promise operates from now — the embalmed moments — and extends into the happy return.

mots.racine: ع م ل / ص ل ح
ط ي ب
ح س ن / أ و ب

versets.ar: كَذَلِكَ أَرْسَلْنَاكَ فِي أُمَّةٍ قَدْ خَلَتْ مِنْ قَبْلِهَا أُمَمٌ لِتَتْلُوَ عَلَيْهِمُ الَّذِي أَوْحَيْنَا إِلَيْكَ وَهُمْ يَكْفُرُونَ بِالرَّحْمَنِ قُلْ هُوَ رَبِّي لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا هُوَ عَلَيْهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ وَإِلَيْهِ مَتَابِ
versets.tr: kadhālika arsalnāka fī ummatin qad khalat min qablihā umamun li-tatluwa ʿalayhimu lladhī awḥaynā ilayka wa-hum yakfurūna bi-r-raḥmāni qul huwa rabbī lā ilāha illā huwa ʿalayhi tawakkaltu wa-ilayhi matābi
versets.trad: Thus have We sent you to a community before whom other communities have passed away, that you may recite to them what We have revealed to you — while they disbelieve in the All-Merciful. Say: “He is my Lord; there is no god but Him; in Him I trust, and to Him is my return.”
versets.commentaire: Consolation and demand. Qushayrī: if We have sent you as prophet to them, We have sent before you many messengers; if a trial reaches you from them, many trials reached those before you — be patient as they were patient, you will be rewarded as they were. If they disbelieve in Us, you believe; and if you believe, do not be troubled by whoever denies, for you are the goal of creation, distinguished by the message and by love.

mots.racine: ر س ل / أ م م
mots.sens: We sent you in a community

mots.racine: ت ل و
ك ف ر / ر ح م
و ك ل / ت و ب
mots.sens: on Him I rely, to Him my return

versets.ar: وَلَوْ أَنَّ قُرْآنًا سُيِّرَتْ بِهِ الْجِبَالُ أَوْ قُطِّعَتْ بِهِ الْأَرْضُ أَوْ كُلِّمَ بِهِ الْمَوْتَى بَلْ لِلَّهِ الْأَمْرُ جَمِيعًا أَفَلَمْ يَيْأَسِ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَنْ لَوْ يَشَاءُ اللَّهُ لَهَدَى النَّاسَ جَمِيعًا وَلَا يَزَالُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا تُصِيبُهُمْ بِمَا صَنَعُوا قَارِعَةٌ أَوْ تَحُلُّ قَرِيبًا مِنْ دَارِهِمْ حَتَّى يَأْتِيَ وَعْدُ اللَّهِ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يُخْلِفُ الْمِيعَادَ
versets.tr: wa-law anna qurʾānan suyyirat bihi l-jibālu aw quṭṭiʿat bihi l-arḍu aw kullima bihi l-mawtā bal li-llāhi l-amru jamīʿan a-fa-lam yayʾasi lladhīna āmanū an law yashāʾu llāhu la-hadā n-nāsa jamīʿan wa-lā yazālu lladhīna kafarū tuṣībuhum bimā ṣanaʿū qāriʿatun aw taḥullu qarīban min dārihim ḥattā yaʾtiya waʿdu llāhi inna llāha lā yukhlifu l-mīʿāda
versets.trad: If a Qurʾān could move mountains, split the earth, or speak to the dead — yet to God belongs the whole affair. Have the believers not realized that, had God willed, He would have guided all mankind? Disasters will not cease to strike the disbelievers for what they have wrought, or settle close to their homes, until God's promise comes. Truly God does not break His promise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī carries the argument to its end: if anything of the created could appear by other than Us in bringing-into-being, it would be by this Qurʾān; but the Originator is God, good and evil — all — come from God, and the Command is entirely God's. Since nothing of the event-bound happens by the Qurʾān — and the Qurʾān is the Speech of God the Mighty — how could an atom of negation or affirmation belong to a creature? That would be impossible. And he comments on a-fa-lam yayʾas: have they not known that whomever the Real guides, that one is the guided?

mots.racine: س ي ر / ج ب ل
mots.sens: mountains would be set in motion by it

mots.racine: ك ل م / م و ت
mots.sens: the dead would be addressed by it

mots.racine: أ م ر / ج م ع
mots.sens: the whole Command belongs to God

mots.racine: ق ر ع
خ ل ف / و ع د

versets.ar: وَلَقَدِ اسْتُهْزِئَ بِرُسُلٍ مِنْ قَبْلِكَ فَأَمْلَيْتُ لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ثُمَّ أَخَذْتُهُمْ فَكَيْفَ كَانَ عِقَابِ
versets.tr: wa-laqadi stuhziʾa bi-rusulin min qablika fa-amlaytu li-lladhīna kafarū thumma akhadhtuhum fa-kayfa kāna ʿiqābi
versets.trad: Messengers before you were mocked; I granted respite to the disbelievers, then I seized them. How was My retribution!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: this verse was sent down as consolation for the Messenger — blessing and peace upon him — against what he was meeting. And just as these have walked the path of their predecessors in denial, We have maintained Our way in punishment. The respite (imlāʾ) granted is never oblivion: it is a delay that ripens the sanction.

mots.racine: ه ز أ / ر س ل
م ل و / ك ف ر
mots.sens: I granted respite to the disbelievers

mots.racine: ع ق ب

versets.ar: أَفَمَنْ هُوَ قَائِمٌ عَلَى كُلِّ نَفْسٍ بِمَا كَسَبَتْ وَجَعَلُوا لِلَّهِ شُرَكَاءَ قُلْ سَمُّوهُمْ أَمْ تُنَبِّئُونَهُ بِمَا لَا يَعْلَمُ فِي الْأَرْضِ أَمْ بِظَاهِرٍ مِنَ الْقَوْلِ بَلْ زُيِّنَ لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مَكْرُهُمْ وَصُدُّوا عَنِ السَّبِيلِ وَمَنْ يُضْلِلِ اللَّهُ فَمَا لَهُ مِنْ هَادٍ
versets.tr: a-fa-man huwa qāʾimun ʿalā kulli nafsin bimā kasabat wa-jaʿalū li-llāhi shurakāʾa qul sammūhum am tunabbiʾūnahu bimā lā yaʿlamu fī l-arḍi am bi-ẓāhirin mina l-qawli bal zuyyina li-lladhīna kafarū makruhum wa-ṣuddū ʿani s-sabīli wa-man yuḍlili llāhu fa-mā lahu min hādin
versets.trad: Is He who stands over every soul observing what it acquires [like any other]? Yet they ascribe partners to God. Say: “Name them! Or would you inform Him of something He does not know on earth — or is it but empty speech?” Rather, their plotting is made attractive to the disbelievers, and they have been kept from the path. Whomever God leads astray has no guide.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the answer is implied: the One who makes flow and originates creation, who rises above them so that none escapes Him — is He like the one who is not so? Never tomorrow will they be equal. On their idolatry: say to them: show me what effect comes from them, what benefit for you in them, what harm for you from them? Would you say what God knows to be otherwise? Plotting embellished in their eyes (zuyyina makruhum) is the worst of veils: they take their deception for clear-sightedness.

mots.racine: ق و م / ن ف س
ك س ب
س م و
ز ي ن / م ك ر
mots.sens: their plot was embellished for them

mots.racine: ه د ي

versets.ar: لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ فِي الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا وَلَعَذَابُ الْآخِرَةِ أَشَقُّ وَمَا لَهُمْ مِنَ اللَّهِ مِنْ وَاقٍ
versets.tr: lahum ʿadhābun fī l-ḥayāti d-dunyā wa-la-ʿadhābu l-ākhirati ashaqqu wa-mā lahum mina llāhi min wāqin
versets.trad: For them is a punishment in the life of this world, and the punishment of the Hereafter is harsher still; they will have no defender against God.
versets.commentaire: Punishment unfolds in two times: here below as warning, heavier still in the hereafter. And no shield (wāq) against God: whoever has deprived himself of His alliance has no protector. The gradation of penalties — already in this world, completed in the next — underlines that disbelief does not wait for the grave to yield its bitter fruits.

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / ح ي ي
أ خ ر / ش ق ق
و ق ي

versets.ar: مَثَلُ الْجَنَّةِ الَّتِي وُعِدَ الْمُتَّقُونَ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهَارُ أُكُلُهَا دَائِمٌ وَظِلُّهَا تِلْكَ عُقْبَى الَّذِينَ اتَّقَوْا وَعُقْبَى الْكَافِرِينَ النَّارُ
versets.tr: mathalu l-jannati llatī wuʿida l-muttaqūna tajrī min taḥtihā l-anhāru ukuluhā dāʾimun wa-ẓilluhā tilka ʿuqbā lladhīna ttaqaw wa-ʿuqbā l-kāfirīna n-nāru
versets.trad: This is the likeness of the Garden promised to the godfearing: rivers flow beneath it, its fruit is perpetual and so is its shade. Such is the final outcome for those who feared God; and the outcome for the disbelievers is the Fire.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: mathal here is the description: the description of the Garden promised to the godfearing is that it is a garden beneath which rivers flow, whose fruit is perpetual and whose shade is perpetual — meaning that the delights in it are continuous. They have gardens immediate and deferred: the deferred is what God has mentioned in the text of the Qurʾān; the immediate is the garden of the instant (jannat al-waqt). And he adds: the degrees, in their unfolding, are continuous there; the breezes of intimacy (nafaḥāt al-uns) for their masters are neither interrupted nor withheld.

mots.racine: م ث ل / ج ن ن
و ع د / و ق ي
أ ك ل / د و م / ظ ل ل
mots.sens: its fruit and shade are perpetual

mots.racine: ع ق ب / ك ف ر / ن و ر
mots.sens: the outcome of disbelievers is the Fire

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ آتَيْنَاهُمُ الْكِتَابَ يَفْرَحُونَ بِمَا أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْكَ وَمِنَ الْأَحْزَابِ مَنْ يُنْكِرُ بَعْضَهُ قُلْ إِنَّمَا أُمِرْتُ أَنْ أَعْبُدَ اللَّهَ وَلَا أُشْرِكَ بِهِ إِلَيْهِ أَدْعُو وَإِلَيْهِ مَآبِ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna ātaynāhumu l-kitāba yafraḥūna bimā unzila ilayka wa-mina l-aḥzābi man yunkiru baʿḍahu qul innamā umirtu an aʿbuda llāha wa-lā ushrika bihi ilayhi adʿū wa-ilayhi maʾābi
versets.trad: Those to whom We have given the Book rejoice in what has been sent down to you; but among the factions are some who deny part of it. Say: “I have been commanded only to worship God and not to associate any with Him. To Him I call, and to Him is my return.”
themes: the joy of the People of the Book
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He means the believers among the People of the Book, who rejoiced at what came down of the Qurʾān, by the sincerity of their certainty. On the servanthood (ʿubūdiyya) commanded: servanthood is the hastening to what is commanded, and the wariness of what is forbidden; then the disowning of force and merit, and the avowal of grace and bounty. The foundation of servanthood is the discharge of the obligations, then uprightness at the moment of the subtle breaths.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ك ت ب
ح ز ب / ن ك ر
mots.sens: among the factions one who denies part

mots.ar: أَنْ أَعْبُدَ اللَّهَ وَلَا أُشْرِكَ بِهِ
mots.tr: an aʿbuda llāha wa-lā ushrika bih
mots.racine: ع ب د / ش ر ك
mots.def: ʿabada: to worship; ashraka: to associate.

mots.racine: د ع و / أ و ب
mots.sens: to Him I call, to Him my return

versets.ar: وَكَذَلِكَ أَنْزَلْنَاهُ حُكْمًا عَرَبِيًّا وَلَئِنِ اتَّبَعْتَ أَهْوَاءَهُمْ بَعْدَمَا جَاءَكَ مِنَ الْعِلْمِ مَا لَكَ مِنَ اللَّهِ مِنْ وَلِيٍّ وَلَا وَاقٍ
versets.tr: wa-kadhālika anzalnāhu ḥukman ʿarabiyyan wa-laʾini ttabaʿta ahwāʾahum baʿdamā jāʾaka mina l-ʿilmi mā laka mina llāhi min waliyyin wa-lā wāqin
versets.trad: Thus have We sent it down as a judgment in Arabic. If you were to follow their desires after the knowledge that has come to you, you would have, against God, neither protector nor defender.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: that is, a judgment in the clear speech of the Arabs, for God sends His messengers in every time in the tongue of their people, that they may be guided to Him. And it is said: among the qualities of the Arabs are bravery, generosity, and respect for the pact of hospitality — all things commended in the Law. And he adds: “if you were to follow their desires” — if you were to accord with them, not entrench yourself in God, and a deference for other than God were to fall upon your heart — you would have no defender against God.

mots.racine: ح ك م / ع ر ب
ت ب ع / ه و ي
ع ل م
و ل ي / و ق ي

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا رُسُلًا مِنْ قَبْلِكَ وَجَعَلْنَا لَهُمْ أَزْوَاجًا وَذُرِّيَّةً وَمَا كَانَ لِرَسُولٍ أَنْ يَأْتِيَ بِآيَةٍ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ اللَّهِ لِكُلِّ أَجَلٍ كِتَابٌ
versets.tr: wa-laqad arsalnā rusulan min qablika wa-jaʿalnā lahum azwājan wa-dhurriyyatan wa-mā kāna li-rasūlin an yaʾtiya bi-āyatin illā bi-idhni llāhi li-kulli ajalin kitābun
versets.trad: We sent messengers before you and gave them wives and offspring. It was not for any messenger to bring a sign except with God's leave. For every term there is a writ.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: We sent before you messengers to their peoples — they were only of your kind; and as you have wives and offspring, they had wives and offspring, without that altering the truth of their mission or those ties distracting them. And he adds, liberating: whoever is occupied with God — the abundance of family and the piling up of occupations do not affect his state, nor do they harm him. On the term: for every thing there is a term inscribed in the Book of God, which is the Preserved; and a moment apportioned to it — no one ascends to His knowledge, no one contradicts His ruling.

mots.racine: ر س ل
ج ع ل / ز و ج / ذ ر ر
mots.sens: We gave them wives and offspring

mots.racine: أ ذ ن
أ ج ل / ك ت ب

versets.ar: يَمْحُو اللَّهُ مَا يَشَاءُ وَيُثْبِتُ وَعِنْدَهُ أُمُّ الْكِتَابِ
versets.tr: yamḥū llāhu mā yashāʾu wa-yuthbitu wa-ʿindahu ummu l-kitābi
versets.trad: God effaces and confirms whatever He wills. With Him is the Mother of the Book.
versets.commentaire: Major verse where Qushayrī unfolds a cascade of effacements: He effaces from the hearts of renouncers the love of this world and confirms in its place the renouncing; He effaces from the hearts of gnostics the personal shares (ḥuẓūẓ) and confirms in their place His rights; He effaces from the hearts of Unifiers the contemplation of the other-than-the-Real and confirms in its place the contemplation of the Real; He effaces the traces of humanity and confirms the lights of contemplating Oneness. And he continues: He effaces the servant from his own attributes and establishes him through the Real — effacement of the creature, establishment by the Real for the Real. As for umm al-kitāb: it is the Preserved Tablet on which He has inscribed what His knowledge and judgment have preceded, without alteration or change; it is also said: a reference to His knowledge encompassing all that can be known.

mots.racine: م ح و / ش ي أ
ث ب ت
أ م م / ك ت ب
mots.sens: with Him is the Mother of the Book
mots.def: umm al-kitāb: Mother of the Book, archetype.

versets.ar: وَإِنْ مَا نُرِيَنَّكَ بَعْضَ الَّذِي نَعِدُهُمْ أَوْ نَتَوَفَّيَنَّكَ فَإِنَّمَا عَلَيْكَ الْبَلَاغُ وَعَلَيْنَا الْحِسَابُ
versets.tr: wa-in mā nuriyannaka baʿḍa lladhī naʿiduhum aw natawaffayannaka fa-innamā ʿalayka l-balāghu wa-ʿalaynā l-ḥisābu
versets.trad: Whether We show you part of what We promise them or take you in death — upon you is only the conveying, and upon Us is the reckoning.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He turns him away from impatience as a command, and writes in their hearts that the promise is near to be made manifest. The division is clear: to the Messenger the conveying (balāgh), to God the reckoning (ḥisāb). This partition frees the messenger from the result: his charge is not conversion, but faithful proclamation.

mots.racine: ر أ ي / و ع د
mots.sens: We show you part of what We promise

mots.racine: و ف ي
ب ل غ
ح س ب

versets.ar: أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا أَنَّا نَأْتِي الْأَرْضَ نَنْقُصُهَا مِنْ أَطْرَافِهَا وَاللَّهُ يَحْكُمُ لَا مُعَقِّبَ لِحُكْمِهِ وَهُوَ سَرِيعُ الْحِسَابِ
versets.tr: a-wa-lam yaraw annā naʾtī l-arḍa nanquṣuhā min aṭrāfihā wa-llāhu yaḥkumu lā muʿaqqiba li-ḥukmihi wa-huwa sarīʿu l-ḥisābi
versets.trad: Do they not see that We come to the land, diminishing it from its outskirts? God judges, and none can reverse His judgment. He is swift in reckoning.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gives several readings: in the commentaries: by the death of the scholars; in the speech of the people of gnosis: by the death of the saints (awliyāʾ) — those to whom people resort when trial strikes them, who invoke God to lift it. And it is said: it is the disappearance of the people of gnosis, so that when a seeker comes upon the path of God, he finds no guide. And it is said: in every age there is a tongue that speaks of the Real — when an intermittence falls, that tongue falls silent: this is the diminishing of the outskirts to which the verse points. On sarīʿ al-ḥisāb: swift in reckoning even in this world, for the saints, as soon as they suffer something or incline to a forbidden, are at once reproached and called to a beautiful return.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ن ق ص / ط ر ف
mots.sens: We come to the land, diminishing its edges

mots.racine: ع ق ب / ح ك م
س ر ع / ح س ب

versets.ar: وَقَدْ مَكَرَ الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ فَلِلَّهِ الْمَكْرُ جَمِيعًا يَعْلَمُ مَا تَكْسِبُ كُلُّ نَفْسٍ وَسَيَعْلَمُ الْكُفَّارُ لِمَنْ عُقْبَى الدَّارِ
versets.tr: wa-qad makara lladhīna min qablihim fa-li-llāhi l-makru jamīʿan yaʿlamu mā taksibu kullu nafsin wa-sa-yaʿlamu l-kuffāru li-man ʿuqbā d-dāri
versets.trad: Those before them plotted, but to God belongs the plot entire. He knows what every soul earns, and the disbelievers shall soon know whose is the final abode.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī seals the sūra with a subtle vision of the divine stratagem: their plot was to feign accord while hiding disbelief; God's plot against them was to leave them imagining themselves benefactors in their acts, believing themselves safe by their states, presuming that their own plot would not catch them. The very fact that He leaves them — with their plotting — is on His part the greatest of plots against them. The sūra closes on this sacred irony: whoever thinks himself to outwit God is already caught in the snare he set for himself.

mots.racine: م ك ر
م ك ر / ج م ع
ع ل م / ك س ب
mots.sens: He knows what every soul earns

mots.racine: ع ق ب / د و ر
